questions.questionText: What do we learn from the report about the fifty-five-year-old woman?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于这位55岁女性，我们从报道中了解到什么？

options.label: A
options.text: She was involved in a conflict with bird lovers.

options.label: B
options.text: She was charged with mistreating animals.

options.label: C
options.text: She was on bad terms with her neighbors.

options.label: D
options.text: She was accused of violating a city law.

questions.questionText: What did the director of the city administration say at the end of the news report?
questions.questionTextZh: 市行政部门主管在新闻报道最后说了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: It will take time to solve the rat problem.

options.label: B
options.text: All wild animals should be well protected.

options.label: C
options.text: The woman was not to blame for the situation.

options.label: D
options.text: No one should go unpunished for violating law.

questions.questionText: What does NASA require the paid volunteers to do?
questions.questionTextZh: NASA要求有偿志愿者做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Communicate with astronauts in Mars Dune Alpha.

options.label: B
options.text: Work in an environment resembling Mars.

options.label: C
options.text: Build a Martian habitat in Houston.

options.label: D
options.text: Send in applications before Friday.

questions.questionText: What will the participants in the project eat?
questions.questionTextZh: 项目参与者将吃什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Ready-made food.

options.label: B
options.text: Food that is organic.

options.label: C
options.text: Food they grow.

options.label: D
options.text: Potatoes mostly.

questions.questionText: What does the news report say about the Japanese mayor Takashi Kawamura?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于日本市长河村孝之，新闻报道说了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: He apologized for scratching an athlete's gold medal.

options.label: B
options.text: He was asked to present a gold medal to Miu Goto.

options.label: C
options.text: He bit a softball player's Olympic gold medal.

options.label: D
options.text: He attracted public attention at a media event.

questions.questionText: What did Mayor Takashi Kawamura offer to do?
questions.questionTextZh: 河村孝之市长主动提出做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Have another picture taken with the Olympic medalist.

options.label: B
options.text: Apologize to the International Olympic Committee.

options.label: C
options.text: Get the damaged medal repaired.

options.label: D
options.text: Pay for the cost of a new medal.

questions.questionText: What did some Olympians say they would do with their medals?
questions.questionTextZh: 一些奥运选手说他们会如何对待他们的奖牌？

options.label: A
options.text: Allow no one to touch them.

options.label: B
options.text: See them as symbols of honor.

options.label: C
options.text: Treat them as treasures.

options.label: D
options.text: Keep them in a safe place.

questions.questionText: What did the woman do to her computer?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士对她的电脑做了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: She covered its screen with a plastic sheet.

options.label: B
options.text: She connected it with her smartphone.

options.label: C
options.text: She decorated it with colorful stickers.

options.label: D
options.text: She bought some new software for it.

questions.questionText: What does the woman say about blue light?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士对蓝光有什么看法？

options.label: A
options.text: It may not be simply blue.

options.label: B
options.text: It includes unnatural light.

options.label: C
options.text: It is more harmful to young people.

options.label: D
options.text: It induces people to fall asleep.

questions.questionText: Why does the man say he hasn't slept enough for months?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士为什么说他几个月来睡眠不足？

options.label: A
options.text: He has had much trouble falling asleep.

options.label: B
options.text: He has had some sort of health problems.

options.label: C
options.text: He has stayed up playing computer games.

options.label: D
options.text: He has been burdened with excessive work.

questions.questionText: What has the woman learned from the research she has read?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士从她读到的研究中学到了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Exposure to blue light is the chief cause of obesity.

options.label: B
options.text: Sleep may be more important than people assumed.

options.label: C
options.text: Sleep may also be negatively affected by natural light.

options.label: D
options.text: Overuse of electronic devices may cause heart disease.

questions.questionText: What question were both speakers fed up with when they were kids?
questions.questionTextZh: 两位说话者小时候都对什么问题感到厌烦？

options.label: A
options.text: What they wanted to be when grown up.

options.label: B
options.text: What their favorite cartoon character was.

options.label: C
options.text: What they learned from computer games.

options.label: D
options.text: What they liked to do most after school.

questions.questionText: What occupation do adults see as both sensible and ambitious according to the woman?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据女士的说法，成年人认为什么职业既务实又有抱负？

options.label: A
options.text: A stock broker.

options.label: B
options.text: A pop singer.

options.label: C
options.text: A mechanical engineer.

options.label: D
options.text: A basketball player.

questions.questionText: What kind of goal setting does the woman like to see in children?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士喜欢看到孩子们设定什么样的目标？

options.label: A
options.text: Ambitious.

options.label: B
options.text: Sensible.

options.label: C
options.text: Imaginative.

options.label: D
options.text: Practical.

questions.questionText: What does the woman suggest adults should do?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士建议成年人应该做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Relax their strict control of their kids.

options.label: B
options.text: Help their kids understand themselves.

options.label: C
options.text: Impose their own dreams on their kids.

options.label: D
options.text: Dismiss their high expectations of their kids.

questions.questionText: What are a number of phone companies trying to do in Africa?
questions.questionTextZh: 许多手机公司在非洲试图做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Set up company branches.

options.label: B
options.text: Improve its infrastructure.

options.label: C
options.text: Introduce iPhones into its markets.

options.label: D
options.text: Promote Internet-ready phones.

questions.questionText: How do KaiOS smartphones differ from smartphones of most other companies?
questions.questionTextZh: KaiOS智能手机与大多数其他公司的智能手机有何不同？

options.label: A
options.text: They cater to Africans' needs.

options.label: B
options.text: They are more expensive models.

options.label: C
options.text: They are more powerful and capable.

options.label: D
options.text: They boast the longest battery life.

questions.questionText: What are KaiOS smartphones equipped with?
questions.questionTextZh: KaiOS智能手机配备了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: A large touchscreen.

options.label: B
options.text: An old-school keypad.

options.label: C
options.text: A voice-response device.

options.label: D
options.text: A digitally-designed system.

questions.questionText: What is said about using recycled plastic to make plastic products in the past?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于过去使用回收塑料制造塑料制品，文章说了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: It ensured sustainable economic growth.

options.label: B
options.text: It was strongly opposed by manufacturers.

options.label: C
options.text: It was cheaper than using fossil fuel plastic.

options.label: D
options.text: It satisfied consumer demands on the whole.

questions.questionText: What has led to a more competitive price for new plastic?
questions.questionTextZh: 什么导致了新塑料价格更具竞争力？

options.label: A
options.text: The capacity to mass produce it.

options.label: B
options.text: The U.S. federal government's regulations.

options.label: C
options.text: A boom in market demand for clear plastic bottles.

options.label: D
options.text: A rapid increase in U.S. petroleum chemical production.

questions.questionText: What does the UK government plan to do about plastic?
questions.questionTextZh: 英国政府计划对塑料采取什么措施？

options.label: A
options.text: Require companies to use 30% of new plastic.

options.label: B
options.text: Increase the supply of new plastic in the market.

options.label: C
options.text: Reduce the amount of plastic pollution in local areas.

options.label: D
options.text: Take measures to promote the use of recycled plastic.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about the Dreamery business in Manhattan?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于曼哈顿的Dreamery业务，我们了解到什么？

options.label: A
options.text: It studies dreams.

options.label: B
options.text: It rents a place for nap-takers.

options.label: C
options.text: It is a hotel for business people.

options.label: D
options.text: It is a nap research institute.

questions.questionText: Why does the speaker ask us to look at the world around us at 2:30 in the afternoon?
questions.questionTextZh: 演讲者为什么让我们在下午2:30看看周围的世界？

options.label: A
options.text: To find out creative people's work performance.

options.label: B
options.text: To see how many people can go without napping.

options.label: C
options.text: To understand the obvious importance of napping.

options.label: D
options.text: To feel how difficult it is to get his idea across.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about the quantity and quality of the speaker's work?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于演讲者工作的数量和质量，我们了解到什么？

options.label: A
options.text: They decline due to pointless meetings.

options.label: B
options.text: They depend on his ability to concentrate.

options.label: C
options.text: They enable him to enjoy a creative career.

options.label: D
options.text: They are affected by the overuse of social media.

questions.questionText: What does the speaker say he finds odd?
questions.questionTextZh: 演讲者说他觉得什么很奇怪？

options.label: A
options.text: Some bosses associate napping with laziness.

options.label: B
options.text: Many office workers nap during work hours.

options.label: C
options.text: Some bosses can concentrate without napping.

options.label: D
options.text: Many of his friends daydream in the office.
